stories.headline: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook
stories.summary: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.

stories.headline: Supreme Court blocks Trump from sacking Fed governor Cook
stories.summary: Justices say president can fire top regulator in separate decision

stories.headline: US says it has agreed deal with Iran to halt strikes and resume talks
stories.summary: White House announces Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will fly to Doha for ‘high-level meetings’ but Tehran disputes that further negotiations have been confirmed

stories.headline: Cook Stays At Fed But Trump Wins Power Over Other Agencies
stories.summary: A closely divided US Supreme Court said Fed Governor Lisa Cook can stay in her job while she fights Trump’s bid to oust her. The ruling reinforced the Federal Reserve’s independence from the White House, for now. Maria Eloisa Capurro reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump's final appeal of E Jean Carroll sex abuse case rejected
stories.summary: The Supreme Court decision means the US president will now have to pay Carroll the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.

stories.headline: In Dissents, Some Justices Push Back Against Ruling to Block Trump From Firing Fed Governor Lisa Cook
stories.summary: A few dissenters cautioned that the majority didn’t allow the legal case over the president’s effort to fire the Fed governor Lisa D. Cook to play out in the lower courts.

stories.headline: Trump Renews Threat to Fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook After Supreme Court Ruling
stories.summary: President Trump promised to “take appropriate action immediately” against Lisa Cook, the Fed governor he had tried to fire, even as the Supreme Court affirmed that Fed officials can be fired only for cause.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Expands Trump’s Power to Fire Officials, but Blocks Fed Governor Dismissal
stories.summary: In twin rulings, the justices said President Trump could fire independent regulators for any reason. But they affirmed the Fed’s independence, and said its leaders could not be fired at will.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Rejects Trump’s Request to Appeal $5 Million Verdict in E. Jean Carroll Case
stories.summary: President Trump had asked the justices to intervene after a jury found that he had sexually abused and defamed the writer E. Jean Carroll.

stories.headline: Supreme Court, for now, blocks Trump from firing Fed board member Lisa Cook
stories.summary: The decision is a setback for Trump, who alleged that Cook committed mortgage fraud. She is the first Black woman to serve on the Fed board.

stories.headline: Supreme Court lets stand $5 million civil verdict against Trump in Carroll case
stories.summary: A 2023 trial found President Donald Trump liable for allegedly sexually assaulting journalist E. Jean Carroll. The decision Monday leaves in place a ruling affirming the judgment.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court backs Trump’s firings; Fed’s Cook reserved as exception
stories.summary: Justices rule in favour of expanded executive power, preserving Lisa Cook’s Fed position amid Trump’s dismissal attempt.

stories.headline: US Supreme Court rebuffs Trump’s appeal in E Jean Carroll case
stories.summary: The US Supreme Court has rejected a push by President Trump to throw out a jury's findings of sexual assault.

stories.headline: Strong aftershock terrifies Venezuelans days after devastating twin quakes
stories.summary: Caracas and La Guaira affected by 4.6-magnitude tremor as death toll passes 1,700 and humanitarian crisis growsA strong aftershock has rattled northern Venezuela, sending terrified residents racing on to the streets five days after the twin earthquakes that killed 1,719 people, left tens of thousands missing and triggered a growing humanitarian emergency.The aftershock early on Monday – which the US Geological Survey measured at a magnitude of 4.

stories.headline: Israeli attacks kill eight people in Gaza and a teenager in the West Bank
stories.summary: Israeli rights group B’Tselem has condemned the 'unprecedented killing of Palestinian children and teenagers"

stories.headline: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
stories.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: Supreme Court protects the Federal Reserve, but what about everybody else?
stories.summary: While the central bank’s independence was upheld, other agencies were not so lucky

stories.headline: Supreme Court Rules Against Roy Moore in Fight Over $8.2 Million Jury Award
stories.summary: The former Alabama Supreme Court justice and Senate candidate had asked the court to clear a path for him to potentially collect a jury award in a defamation case.

stories.headline: EU sets up three months of talks with China over €360bn trade deficit
stories.summary: Two sides agree to try to make bilateral relationship ‘more balanced’ after weeks of threatsThe EU and China have agreed to enter three months of talks to try to avoid a trade war over the bloc’s €360bn (£310bn) annual import/export imbalance.In their first joint statement in seven years, the two sides agreed in Brussels to open a formal trade consultation after weeks of threats and recriminations from China if the EU imposed any measures to stop

stories.headline: Afghan families mourn loved ones as border tensions with Pakistan rise
stories.summary: Afghan officials say Pakistani attacks killed at least 36 civilians, while Pakistan says its forces killed 29 fighters.

stories.headline: South African leader warns anti-migrant protesters ahead of unofficial deadline
stories.summary: Thousands of people from other African countries have left South Africa ahead of Tuesday's deadline set by anti-migrant groups.

stories.headline: DR Congo bans mass gatherings in the  capital to prevent spread of Ebola
stories.summary: Opposition politicians accuse the government of using the outbreak to halt a planned protest.

stories.headline: Teyana Taylor's tears and Lauryn Hill tribute steal show at BET Awards
stories.summary: Taylor had an emotional reaction to being presented with the Icon of the Year award by Janet Jackson.

stories.headline: Senior South African police officer survives assassination attempt
stories.summary: Deputy crime intelligence boss Maj-Gen Feroz Khan was due to appear before an inquiry into police corruption this week.

stories.headline: Fired F.T.C. Commissioner Warns of Potential for Presidential Abuse of Power
stories.summary: Rebecca Slaughter said independent agencies like the Federal Trade Commission were created as watchdogs of powerful corporations and that presidents shouldn’t interfere with that.

stories.headline: Why Trump Is Giving Republicans Midterms Anxiety
stories.summary: Ahead of the midterms, Republicans have sought to focus on the issue of affordability, but the president keeps going off script. Our White House correspondent Tyler Pager explains.

stories.headline: Why resisting Trump has galvanized Black Democrats as the midterms approach
stories.summary: High primary turnout among Black voters in the South has given some in the party hope for upset wins in the region.

stories.headline: Shares in chipmakers underpinning AI boom rocket in first half of 2026

stories.headline: Europe heatwave shows need to reject climate denial ‘lies’, says EU green chief

stories.headline: Pakistani strikes kill at least 28 civilians in Afghanistan, UN says
stories.summary: Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".

stories.headline: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
stories.summary: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

stories.headline: South Korea unveils $880bn chip and AI investment plan
stories.summary: It comes as regional rivals like Taiwan, China and Japan are investing heavily in chip factories and other technology.

stories.headline: Dogs, drones and sound detectors: How rescuers search for quake survivors
stories.summary: Teams armed with a range of techniques continue to dig through the rubble in Venezuela.

stories.summary: American companies rush to stockpile goods ahead of fresh round of levies

stories.headline: The Supreme Court Contradicted Itself
stories.summary: The Supreme Court has issued two opinions that affect the independence of the US Federal Reserve, creating an extremely high-risk strategy, explains Bloomberg Opinion columnist and Harvard Law professor Noah Feldman. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Yen Hits 40-Year Low in Historic Slide That’s Rattled Japan
stories.summary: The yen slid to its weakest level against the dollar since 1986, a milestone that will generate unease in Japan and put traders on high alert for authorities wading into the market.

stories.headline: Lagarde Says European Banking System More Resilient to Shocks
stories.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde says Europe is becoming less vulnerable to outside shocks thanks to a better financial framework and progress on the green transition. She speaks during her institution’s annual retreat in Sintra, Portugal. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Ethics Panel Tosses Misconduct Claims Against Gallego
stories.summary: The Ethics Committee told Senator Ruben Gallego it had dismissed allegations he engaged in sexual misconduct and campaign finance violations.

stories.headline: See How the Makeup of Independent Agencies Has Changed Under Trump
stories.summary: Even before the Supreme Court granted him more power to fire officials, President Trump had effectively ended Democratic majorities at several agencies.

stories.headline: Undocumented migrants flee South Africa amid rising anti-immigrant protests
stories.summary: Thousands of undocumented migrants in South Africa are rushing to leave after anti-immigrant protests.

stories.headline: Burnham pledges devolution and discipline if he becomes UK prime minister
stories.summary: The frontrunner to succeed Keir Starmer was criticised for not taking questions after setting out his policy vision

stories.headline: Fire and floods hit Ghana’s capital as residents are left stranded
stories.summary: A massive fire engulfed a rubber factory in Ghana’s capital, Accra, as severe flooding swamped parts of the city.

stories.headline: Earthquake jolts southwest China
stories.summary: An earthquake with a magnitude of 5.5 struck Yibin in China’s southwest province of Sichuan shortly on Monday.

stories.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

stories.summary: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.headline: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes
stories.summary: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
stories.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
stories.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

stories.headline: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
stories.summary: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.

stories.headline: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
stories.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
stories.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

stories.headline: Trump accuses Iran of ceasefire breach after Strait of Hormuz attack
stories.summary: A Singapore-flagged ship was struck crossing the waterway on Thursday, prompting a large-scale evacuation to be paused.

stories.summary: Iran's coach refuses to discuss pride celebrations in Seattle, where his country face Egypt in a crucial Group G match.

stories.headline: Would farmers and fishermen back Brexit today?
stories.summary: Polls suggested the majority of farmers and fishers in England voted to leave the EU 10 years ago.

stories.headline: Poland's Tusk calls for 'mutual respect' during row with Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukraine's Volodmyr Zelensky skips major conference aimed at rebuilding his country after being stripped of a Polish state honour.

stories.headline: UPS is losing ground to FedEx
stories.summary: The two logistics giants have similar strategies—and face a common threat

stories.headline: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

stories.headline: Sydney woman wakes from induced coma more than a week after shark attack

stories.headline: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
stories.summary: Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation.

stories.headline: Leading Pakistan activist given life sentence over soldier's killing at rally
stories.summary: Human rights campaigner Mahrang Baloch denied inciting protesters before the official's death.
